skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa via inference.sh CLI. Apps: Tavily Search, Tavily Extract, Exa Search, Exa Answer, Exa Extract. Capabilities: AI-powered search, content extraction, direct answers, research. Use for: research, RAG pipelines, fact-checking, content aggregation, agents. Triggers: web search, tavily, exa, search api, content extraction, research, internet search, ai search, search assistant, web scraping, rag, perplexity alternative
skill.summary: Enable AI assistants to search the web and extract content using Tavily and Exa APIs through the inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: All 38 static findings evaluated as false positives. HIGH findings (Weak cryptographic algorithm) were incorrectly flagging legitimate YAML/table syntax. MEDIUM findings (Ruby/shell backtick execution) incorrectly identified standard CLI commands with --input flags. LOW findings (Hardcoded URL) are legitimate documentation links. The skill is a standard web search tool with no malicious intent or security concerns.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged YAML description fields and table formatting as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. The word 'Extract' in content extraction context was incorrectly identified. No cryptographic code exists in this file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by manual review - all flagged lines contain legitimate YAML metadata and documentation, not cryptographic algorithms.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution Detection
medium_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly identified belt CLI commands with --input flags as 'backtick execution'. The code uses standard CLI invocation syntax, not shell backticks. No backtick syntax (`) exists in this file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual code review confirms all commands use standard CLI flags (--input, --output) without backtick syntax. The scanner confuses command structure detection with actual execution patterns.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged documentation URLs as 'hardcoded URLs'. All URLs point to legitimate inference.sh platform documentation and example domains. No malicious endpoints or credential exfiltration targets detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs verified as legitimate documentation links to inference.sh domain. No credential harvesting or data exfiltration URLs present.

content.user_title: Search the web and extract content with AI
content.value_statement: AI assistants cannot access current information from the internet. This skill enables web search and content extraction using Tavily and Exa APIs through the inference.sh CLI, allowing Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to gather real-time information and research any topic.
actual_capabilities: Search the web using Tavily AI-powered search and receive answers with sources
Extract clean content from multiple URLs simultaneously
Search using Exa for highly relevant links with context
Get direct factual answers to questions via Exa Answer
Extract and analyze web page content with Exa Extract
Chain search results with LLM processing for research workflows
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (belt) installed and authenticated
API rate limits apply based on Tavily and Exa subscription tiers
Does not provide browsing or interactive web navigation

use_cases.title: Research and fact-checking
use_cases.description: Ask the AI to search for current information on any topic and receive well-sourced answers with links to verify claims.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers, journalists, and analysts who need accurate, sourced information

use_cases.title: RAG pipeline data gathering
use_cases.description: Search for and extract content from multiple sources to build knowledge bases for retrieval-augmented generation.
use_cases.target_user: AI developers building RAG systems and knowledge bases

use_cases.title: Content aggregation for agents
use_cases.description: Equip AI agents with search capabilities to gather information autonomously during task execution.
use_cases.target_user: AI agent developers and automation engineers

prompt_templates.title: Quick web search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for [topic] and summarize the key findings.

prompt_templates.title: Research with sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for information about [topic]. Find the most relevant sources and provide a summary with citations.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-source extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [topic] and extract the main content from the top 3 results. Summarize the key points from each source.

prompt_templates.title: Research workflow with analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: First search for recent developments in [topic]. Then analyze the findings and identify the main trends. Finally, provide a detailed report with sources.

output_examples.input: Search for latest developments in quantum computing
output_examples.output:
Based on recent searches, here are the key developments in quantum computing:

1. **IBM's 1000+ qubit processor** - IBM unveiled their latest quantum processor with over 1000 qubits, marking significant progress in quantum computing scale.

2. **Error correction breakthroughs** - New error correction techniques have improved qubit reliability by 40%.

3. **Quantum networking** - Progress in quantum entanglement distribution over 100+ km distances.

Sources: IBM Research Blog, Nature Physics, Quantum Computing Report

output_examples.input: Extract content from https://example.com/research-paper and summarize
output_examples.output:
**Article Summary: Understanding Quantum Computing Applications**

The paper discusses practical applications of quantum computing in drug discovery, financial modeling, and cryptography.

Key findings:
- Quantum computing could accelerate drug discovery by simulating molecular interactions
- Financial institutions are exploring quantum optimization for portfolio management
- Post-quantum cryptography is becoming critical for data security

The article concludes that quantum computing will have transformative impact across industries within the next decade.

best_practices: Authenticate with inference.sh CLI before using the skill to ensure API access
Use specific, targeted queries for better search results rather than broad questions
Chain search operations with LLM calls for comprehensive research workflows
anti_patterns: Do not use for real-time interactive browsing - this skill provides search and extraction, not full web navigation
Do not make excessive rapid requests - respect API rate limits
Do not assume search results are always accurate - verify critical information with primary sources

faq.question: How do I install and set up the web search skill?
faq.answer: Install the inference.sh CLI (belt) by following the instructions at the provided install link. Then run 'belt login' to authenticate with your API credentials. Once set up, you can use the skill with commands like 'belt app run tavily/search-assistant'.

faq.question: What is the difference between Tavily and Exa?
faq.answer: Tavily focuses on AI-powered search with built-in answer generation and source citations. Exa provides semantic search with highly relevant links and supports direct factual answers. Use Tavily for research with citations, Exa for finding specific content and links.

faq.question: Can I extract content from multiple URLs at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Tavily Extract or Exa Extract apps with a JSON array of URLs. This allows batch extraction from multiple web pages in a single operation.

faq.question: What are the API rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your inference.sh subscription tier. Free tier has limited requests per minute, while paid tiers offer higher limits. Check the inference.sh documentation for specific limits.

faq.question: How can I combine search with AI analysis?
faq.answer: Use workflow patterns that chain search commands with LLM processing. First run a search to gather information, save results, then feed them to a language model for analysis and summarization.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for real-time news and current events?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill searches the live web so results include current information. Both Tavily and Exa index recent content, making this suitable for news, current events, and time-sensitive research.
